capture_note: Captured from the packaged Next.js dashboard served by agent-bom serve on current main, with bundled demo data pushed through /v1/results/push. Graph screenshots are real product routes in capture mode; README proof now shows focused graph modes instead of one all-in mesh canvas. Fleet and identity frames use synthetic seeded control-plane records written through /v1/fleet and /v1/identities so the UI shows environment review state and IAM lifecycle evidence without claiming that the local demo scan alone discovers those records. The gateway policy frame was captured from the same SQLite capture store after seeding one advisory baseline policy and one dry-run evaluation.

screenshots.scope: Risk overview top frame with headline KPIs, posture grade, and start of attack paths
Risk overview mid-frame with attack paths and exposure KPIs
Focused agent mesh graph across the active agent, MCP server, package, tool, and CVE path, cropped to the product graph surface
Runtime gateway policy posture with one advisory baseline policy, two rules, and two bound agents
Fix-first attack path queue with snapshot pressure, graph evidence export, and remediation handoff
Root-centered lineage investigation with reachable node counts, bounded paths, filters, and graph evidence export
Agent-scoped context map showing reachable MCP servers and lateral movement side panel
Expanded fleet row showing lifecycle distribution, approved state, owner metadata, environment label, and discovery state
Audit log filtered to identity resources with HMAC integrity counters and agent identity issue, rotate, revoke events
Supply chain dependency map with scan pipeline counts and package risk distribution
Fix-first remediation table with prioritized packages and framework context
